note: REVIEW PROPOSAL — not applied. D1 is canonical; disk lags. Apply approved changes via the Worker API (POST /api/article/:slug with base_version), keyed by (slug, annotation id). Suggestions are human-gated: a unique last-segment match is a hint, not a proof.

overclaims_proof_wanted_badged_formalize.label: Homotopy form of the Poincaré conjecture
Exotic R^4: uncountably many non-diffeomorphic smooth structures

annotations.label: Periodic functions have discrete spectra
annotations.note: Discreteness is implicit in representing periodic functions by ℤ-indexed Fourier coefficients, but there is no theorem stating periodicity implies a discrete spectrum.

annotations.label: Fourier series
annotations.note: fourierCoeff and the series ∑ fourierCoeff n • fourier n give the Fourier series representation, shown to sum to f when coefficients are summable.

annotations.label: Sufficient condition for recovery (dual of Nyquist–Shannon)
annotations.note: Mathlib gives summability of Fourier coefficients as a sufficient condition for recovery, but not the bandlimited dual-of-Nyquist sampling condition.

annotations.label: Category of abelian groups Ab
annotations.note: An `Abelian` instance is registered on `AddCommGrpCat`.

annotations.label: Similarity class
annotations.note: Mathlib has a notion of simplex similarity in `Mathlib.Geometry.Euclidean.Similarity`, but does not bundle similarity classes as a set.

annotations.label: Affine geometry
annotations.note: Mathlib develops affine geometry via the `AffineSpace` notation (an `AddTorsor`) but has no encompassing 'affine geometry' object.

annotations.label: Affine space via linear algebra
annotations.note: `AffineSpace V P` is a scoped notation for `AddTorsor V P`, exactly the linear-algebra approach.

annotations.label: Affine space (coordinatized)
annotations.note: `AffineSpace V P` with `V` a module over a ring/field captures the coordinatized affine space.

annotations.label: Affine space
annotations.note: Mathlib's AffineSpace is the AddTorsor notion; the AG affine space A^n underlying MvPolynomial.zeroLocus (σ → K) is not a named object.

annotations.label: Affine geometry
annotations.note: AffineSpace is a scoped notation for AddTorsor and a full affine geometry library exists, capturing the field but not a single 'affine geometry' statement.

annotations.label: Affine geometry
annotations.note: `AffineSpace` (notation for `AddTorsor`) formalizes the central object, not the informal disciplinary definition.

annotations.label: Tensor product of algebras
annotations.note: `Algebra.TensorProduct R A B` gives the algebra tensor product.

annotations.label: Parallelotope volume formula
annotations.note: `Basis.addHaar_parallelepiped` gives the parallelepiped volume as `ENNReal.ofReal |b.det v|`.

annotations.label: Bi-orthogonality property
annotations.note: `Basis.dualBasis_apply_self` states `b.dualBasis i (b j) = if j = i then 1 else 0`, the Kronecker-delta biorthogonality.

annotations.label: Matrix biorthogonality of basis and dual basis
annotations.note: The biorthogonality `dualBasis_apply_self` is formalized, but the explicit statement about inverse coordinate matrices of basis vs dual basis is not packaged as such.

annotations.label: Coordinate isomorphism from a basis
annotations.note: `Basis.equivFun` is the linear equiv V ≃ₗ (ι → R) for a finite basis (`Basis.repr` is the general Finsupp version).

annotations.label: Every vector space has a basis
annotations.note: `Basis.ofVectorSpace K V` produces a basis of any vector space over a division ring.

annotations.label: Basis of a vector space via transfinite recursion
annotations.note: `Basis.ofVectorSpace` produces a basis for an arbitrary vector space (the result of the construction), though Mathlib's proof uses Zorn's lemma rather than explicit transfinite recursion.

annotations.label: V isomorphic to V* but not naturally
annotations.note: `Basis.toDualEquiv` gives `M ≃ₗ[R] Dual R M` from a finite basis; the 'no natural isomorphism' caveat is informal/not formalized.

annotations.label: Every monad arises from an adjunction
annotations.note: The Eilenberg–Moore adjunction `Monad.adj : T.free ⊣ T.forget` realizes any monad, with `Monad.adjToMonadIso : T.adj.toMonad ≅ T`.

annotations.label: Hom left-exact, exact iff projective
annotations.note: `coyoneda.obj (op P)` always preserves limits (`coyoneda_preservesLimits`, i.e. left-exactness), and `projective_iff_preservesEpimorphisms_coyoneda_obj` gives `Projective P ↔ Hom(P,–) preserves epis` for general categories.

annotations.label: Every small category is concretizable
annotations.note: The faithful Yoneda embedding `C ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type v₁` is proven, but no theorem packages a small `C` as a `ConcreteCategory`.

annotations.label: Faithful functor from presheaf category
annotations.note: Yoneda gives a faithful (in fact fully faithful) `C ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type v₁`, but no faithful functor `[Cᵒᵖ, Set] → Set` is stated.

annotations.label: Exact sequence for Picard group
annotations.note: Component maps and quotient isomorphisms (e.g. `unitsToPic`, `unitsQuotEquivRelPic`) exist, but the named units–fractions–Picard exact sequence is not assembled.

annotations.label: Projective limit of rings
annotations.note: Categorical limits in `CommRingCat`/`RingCat` exist; no separately bundled 'projective limit of rings' constructor.

annotations.label: Compact elements of arithmetic lattice
annotations.note: `IsCompactElement` is defined and `isCompactElement_finsetSup` exists, but the lattice structure on the set of compact elements in arithmetic lattices is not packaged as a single declaration.

annotations.label: Embedding into completion defines an isometry onto dense subspace
annotations.note: Mathlib proves `Completion.coe_isometry : Isometry (coe : alpha -> Completion alpha)` and `Completion.denseRange_coe : DenseRange (coe : alpha -> Completion alpha)`.

annotations.label: Natural logarithm is analytic on single-valued branches
annotations.note: `analyticAt_clog` proves the principal branch of `Complex.log` is analytic on the slit plane; `analyticAt_log` covers real positive `x`.

annotations.label: Logarithm continuation around the origin
annotations.note: `Complex.log` is analytic on the slit plane via `analyticAt_clog`, but the multi-valued continuation around 0 isn't formalized.

annotations.label: Complex exponential is entire
annotations.note: Complex.analyticOnNhd_cexp shows exp is analytic on all of ℂ (also Complex.differentiable_exp), i.e. entire.

annotations.label: Euclidean vector space structure
annotations.note: ℂ carries the instance `InnerProductSpace ℝ ℂ` and `finrank ℝ ℂ = 2`, making it a 2-dimensional Euclidean space.

annotations.label: Halting problem is NP-hard not NP-complete
annotations.note: The halting problem's undecidability is formalized as `halting_problem`, but Mathlib does not state its NP-hardness or non-NP-completeness.

annotations.label: Undecidability of normal form
annotations.note: Mathlib has Rice's theorem for partial recursive functions (`Computability.rice` in `Mathlib/Computability/Halting.lean`), but no statement of undecidability specific to combinatory-logic normal forms.

annotations.label: Analogue of Rice's theorem
annotations.note: The classical Rice's theorem is in `Mathlib/Computability/Halting.lean` as `Computability.rice`, but its CL analogue is not stated.

annotations.label: Projective planes exist for prime power orders
annotations.note: Mathlib supplies a `ProjectivePlane` instance for ℙ K (Fin 3 → K), giving prime-power-order planes via finite fields, but no general existence statement.

annotations.label: Metric spaces are paracompact, Hausdorff, normal
annotations.note: Metric spaces are paracompact and T2, hence normal (T4Space.of_paracompactSpace_t2Space).

annotations.label: A_n normal of index 2
annotations.note: alternatingGroup.normal gives normality and alternatingGroup.index_eq_two gives index 2 for nontrivial α.

annotations.label: Klein four-group in S_4
annotations.note: Mathlib defines the Klein four subgroup V and proves it normal/characteristic inside alternatingGroup (Fin 4), not directly as a normal subgroup of S_4.

annotations.label: Inverse matrix convention
annotations.note: `transpose_permMatrix` gives `(σ.permMatrix)ᵀ = σ⁻¹.permMatrix`, the math behind the inverse convention, but the notational discussion is informal.

annotations.label: Symmetric group
annotations.note: `Equiv.permGroup` gives the group structure on `Equiv.Perm α`, i.e. the symmetric group.

annotations.label: Symmetric group structure
annotations.note: `Equiv.permGroup` provides the symmetric-group structure on permutations of a finite set.

annotations.label: Composition satisfies group axioms
annotations.note: `Equiv.permGroup` is the `Group` instance for permutations under composition.

annotations.label: Perpendicular to radius at endpoint is tangent
annotations.note: Direct converse: `∠ q p s.center = π/2` and `p ∈ s` implies `s.IsTangentAt p line[ℝ, p, q]`.

annotations.label: Acute angle
annotations.note: Acuteness (angle < π/2) is defined only as the AcuteAngled property of simplices, not as a standalone acute-angle predicate.

annotations.label: Thales' theorem (overview)
annotations.note: Aliased as thales_theorem: the angle inscribed in a semicircle (on a diameter) is a right angle.

annotations.label: Thales' theorem
annotations.note: Directly formalizes Thales' theorem about the right angle subtended by a diameter.

annotations.label: Thales' theorem
annotations.note: Thales' theorem (angle in a semicircle is right) is formalized with an explicit alias.

annotations.label: Reformulation via sequences
annotations.note: `exists_seq_tendsto_limsup`/`liminf` give sequence-based realisations, but a full bidirectional reformulation theorem is not isolated.

annotations.label: Combinatorial number system bijection
annotations.note: Mathlib has colex ordering of finsets (`Finset.Colex`, `orderIsoColex` for all subsets) but not the explicit combinadic bijection from `[0, choose n k)` to k-combinations.

annotations.label: Sum of powers for large n (Kerala)
annotations.note: Faulhaber's formula (Finset.sum_range_pow) gives exact power sums via Bernoulli numbers, but not the Kerala asymptotic ∑iᵏ ≈ nᵏ⁺¹/(k+1).

annotations.label: Sum of fourth powers
annotations.note: The general Faulhaber theorem Finset.sum_range_pow (via Bernoulli numbers) is formalized, but the specific closed-form polynomial for the sum of fourth powers is not stated.

annotations.label: Dedekind finite phrasing
annotations.note: Mathlib has the finite injective-iff-surjective equivalences capturing Dedekind-finiteness, but not this exact compound phrasing about non-injective surjections.

annotations.label: Finite equal-size: surjective iff injective
annotations.note: injective_iff_surjective_of_equiv proves Injective f ↔ Surjective f for f between finite types of equal cardinality.

annotations.label: Morley's categoricity theorem
annotations.note: `Categorical` and the Łoś–Vaught test (`Categorical.isComplete`) exist, but Morley's uncountable-categoricity transfer theorem is not proved.

annotations.label: Realising types in an elementary extension
annotations.note: Mathlib proves every complete type is realised in some model of T, but not the stronger statement constructing an elementary extension of a given structure realizing a set of types.

annotations.label: Löwenheim–Skolem theorems
annotations.note: Both the upward (this decl) and downward (`exists_elementarySubstructure_card_eq`) Löwenheim–Skolem theorems are formalized.

annotations.label: Grothendieck group
annotations.note: The Grothendieck group construction and universal property exist, but group completion as a left adjoint to the forgetful functor is not formalized as an adjunction.

annotations.label: Morphisms of algebraic structures
annotations.note: Mathlib supplies bundled categories like `Grp`, `Ring`, `CommRingCat`, `ModuleCat` whose morphisms are the corresponding structure-preserving homomorphisms.

annotations.label: Laplace equation
annotations.note: Mathlib defines the Laplacian Δ (InnerProductSpace.laplacian) and HarmonicAt f x as Δf =ᶠ 0, i.e. exactly the solutions of the Laplace equation.

annotations.label: Harmonic functions
annotations.note: Defined as C^2 with Laplacian vanishing in a neighborhood, alongside `HarmonicOnNhd`.

annotations.label: Finite direct product as direct sum of ideals
annotations.note: Decomposition via complete orthogonal idempotents is formalized; the precise direct-sum-of-ideals identification is not a single named lemma.

annotations.label: Product property
annotations.note: A product formula χ(α×β)=χ(α)χ(β) exists for bounded finite posets, but the topological version for product spaces/manifolds is not formalized.

annotations.label: Laplace's equation in spherical coordinates
annotations.note: Mathlib defines the Laplacian and harmonic functions (Δf = 0), but not the spherical-coordinate expression.

annotations.label: Infinite cyclic group
annotations.note: The instance `IsAddCyclic ℤ` realizes ℤ as the infinite cyclic group.

annotations.label: Integers under addition
annotations.note: `IsAddCyclic ℤ` is registered as an instance with `1` as generator.

annotations.label: Dimension inequality for Noetherian local rings
annotations.note: `ringKrullDim_le_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal` gives dim R ≤ minimal generators of m, equal to finrank of the cotangent space.

annotations.label: Iterate bound for the Picard operator (Γᵐ)
annotations.note: Mathlib proves `dist ((next)^[n] α t) ((next)^[n] β t) ≤ (K·|t−t₀|)ⁿ / n! · dist α β`, which is the article's lemma `‖Γᵐφ₁(t)−Γᵐφ₂(t)‖ ≤ Lᵐ|t−t₀|ᵐ/m! · ‖φ₁−φ₂‖`.

annotations.label: Picard operator is a contraction (after restricting the interval)
annotations.note: Mathlib proves that some iterate `(next hf hx)^[n]` is a `ContractingWith C`, which suffices to apply Banach fixed-point and is strictly more general than directly contracting Γ.

annotations.label: Solutions of the ODE satisfy the integral equation
annotations.note: Mathlib's `picard_eq_of_hasDerivAt` proves exactly this implication: if α has the right derivative on [t₀, t], then `picard f t₀ (α t₀) α t = α t` (i.e. α equals its own Picard image). The converse is `hasDerivWithinAt_picard_Icc`, and `IsP

annotations.label: 1 as probability of a certain event
annotations.note: IsProbabilityMeasure requires measure_univ : μ univ = 1, i.e. the certain event (whole space) has probability 1.

annotations.label: Quotient of compact Hausdorff equivalences
annotations.note: Related results exist (`IsQuotientMap.of_surjective_continuous` for compact-to-Hausdorff maps), but the TFAE for quotient maps out of a compact Hausdorff space is not formalized as such.

annotations.label: Mazur–Ulam theorem
annotations.note: An isometric bijection between real normed (affine) spaces is affine, via the Mazur-Ulam conversions (toRealLinearIsometryEquiv / toRealAffineIsometryEquiv).

annotations.label: Reciprocal via Möbius function
annotations.note: `LSeries_zeta_mul_Lseries_moebius` proves ζ(s)·∑μ(n)n^(−s) = 1 for Re s > 1, equivalent to 1/ζ(s) = ∑μ(n)n^(−s).

annotations.label: Root system of SL(2, R)
annotations.note: `Mathlib.Algebra.Lie.Sl2` develops sl₂-triples, but the explicit A₁ root system computation for sl(2,ℝ) is not given.

annotations.label: Linear programming
annotations.note: Only an internal simplex-algorithm oracle for the linarith tactic exists; linear programming theory (duality, optima) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Lindemann's transcendence of pi
annotations.note: Only the analytical part of Lindemann's theorem is formalized; transcendence of π itself is not yet in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Minkowski norm squared
annotations.note: The quadratic form v ↦ B(v,v) of a bilinear form is general infrastructure, but the Minkowski norm squared is not defined.

annotations.label: Operator norm formula
annotations.note: The operator norm of a symmetric operator equals the supremum of the absolute Rayleigh quotient.

annotations.label: Locally path-connected implies locally connected
annotations.note: Anonymous `instance : LocallyConnectedSpace X` at LocPathConnected.lean:170 derives local connectedness from local path-connectedness.

annotations.label: Lucas–Lehmer primality test
annotations.note: The Lucas-Lehmer test is formalized (`lucas_lehmer_sufficiency`).

annotations.label: Characterization of inner products on R^n via symmetric positive-definite matrices
annotations.note: `Matrix.toInnerProductSpace` constructs the inner product on `n → 𝕜` induced by a positive-(semi)definite matrix.

annotations.label: Hermitian form on complex coordinate space
annotations.note: `Matrix.toInnerProductSpace` (over `RCLike 𝕜 = ℂ`) gives the general `⟪x, y⟫ = xᴴ M y` form on `n → ℂ`.

annotations.label: Dot product as a tensor
annotations.note: `Matrix.dotProductBilin` packages the dot product as a bilinear map, i.e. a (0,2)-tensor.

annotations.label: Gaussian elimination over any field
annotations.note: The transvection reduction to diagonal form is proved for matrices over an arbitrary field, capturing the field-generality claim.

annotations.label: Eigenvalues are diagonal entries
annotations.note: Only the diagonal-matrix case (eigenvalues are the diagonal entries) is formalized; the triangular/Jordan version is not.

annotations.label: Matrix ring over a division ring is semisimple
annotations.note: Matrix rings over a division ring are shown semisimple in `WedderburnArtin`.

annotations.label: Properties of outer product of vectors
annotations.note: Bilinearity is packaged as `vecMulVecBilin`, with `add_vecMulVec`, `vecMulVec_add`, `smul_vecMulVec`, `transpose_vecMulVec` covering the listed algebraic identities.

annotations.label: Existence of semi-invariant measures on homogeneous spaces
annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes Haar quotient measures via fundamental domains; the general modular-character criterion for semi-invariant measures is not stated as such.

annotations.label: Cartan's functional construction of Haar measure
annotations.note: Mathlib's construction uses `haarContent` on compacts rather than Cartan's compactly-supported-function infimum, but yields the same Haar measure.

annotations.label: Fractal (self-similarity)
annotations.note: Mathlib defines Hausdorff dimension (the mathematical notion of fractal dimension) but has no definition of a fractal or self-similar set.

annotations.label: Fractal dimension
annotations.note: Mathlib has no abstract notion of "fractal dimension" but does formalize a concrete instance, the Hausdorff dimension `MeasureTheory.dimH`.

annotations.label: Hausdorff dimension
annotations.note: `MeasureTheory.dimH s` is defined and `dimH_def` shows it equals `⨆ d, (μH[d] s = ∞) → d`, matching the Wikipedia definition.

annotations.label: Essential supremum convention
annotations.note: `essSup f μ` is defined as `(ae μ).limsup f`, capturing the essential supremum of a measurable function.

annotations.label: Expectation via probability density function
annotations.note: `integral_pdf_smul` expresses `∫ f x · pdf X x dx = ∫ f ∘ X dℙ`, the density-based formula for expectation of `f(X)`.

annotations.label: Integral of a complex-valued function
annotations.note: `integral_re_add_im` proves ∫ f = (∫ re f) + i · (∫ im f); the Bochner `integral` already handles ℂ-valued functions directly.

annotations.label: Kolmogorov's existence theorem
annotations.note: Kolmogorov's extension theorem is realized via `isProjectiveLimit_infinitePi`, constructing the projective-limit measure for product spaces (also see `isProjectiveLimit_infinitePiNat`).

annotations.label: Modular function is a continuous homomorphism
annotations.note: Mathlib defines `modularCharacter : G →* ℝ≥0` (homomorphism property formalized); continuity as a function is not asserted in the file.

annotations.label: Modular function (Haar modulus)
annotations.note: `modularCharacterFun g` is defined so that `map (· * g) μ = modularCharacterFun g • μ` for a left Haar measure.

annotations.label: Classical definition of probability
annotations.note: The equiprobable assignment is realized by the uniform measure `uniformOn`, though the philosophical 'classical definition' itself is not stated.

annotations.label: Classical probability formula
annotations.note: `uniformOn_univ` gives `uniformOn univ s = Measure.count s / Fintype.card`, i.e. favorable-over-total for equally likely outcomes.

annotations.label: Area under a curve
annotations.note: Area between a positive curve and the axis is the f=0 case of the region-between-graphs integral.

annotations.label: Area between two graphs
annotations.note: `volume_regionBetween_eq_integral` gives the measure of the region between f and g as ∫(g−f).

annotations.label: Hölder's inequality on a probability space
annotations.note: Mathlib has the underlying pieces (Hölder + `MemLp.mono_exponent` for finite measures) but no single decl stating the relaxed `1/p + 1/q ≤ 1` Hölder on a probability space.

annotations.label: Absolute moment inequality
annotations.note: `MemLp.mono_exponent` shows that for a finite measure (in particular a probability measure), `MemLp f q μ` and `p ≤ q` imply `MemLp f p μ`, i.e. the lower absolute moment is finite.

annotations.label: Double annihilator and Galois connection (finite-dim)
annotations.note: `dualAnnihilator_gc` is the antitone Galois connection; `Subspace.dualAnnihilator_dualAnnihilator` gives the double annihilator via `mapEvalEquiv`.

annotations.label: Dual of infinite-dimensional space has larger dimension
annotations.note: `lift_rank_lt_rank_dual` states the rank of an infinite-dimensional space is strictly less than that of its dual.

annotations.label: Erdős–Kaplansky theorem
annotations.note: The Erdős–Kaplansky theorem is stated as `rank_dual_eq_card_dual_of_aleph0_le_rank`.

annotations.label: Rank zero iff torsion
annotations.note: `rank_eq_zero_iff_isTorsion` states rank 0 ↔ torsion for modules over a domain, which specializes to ℤ-modules.

annotations.label: Rank 0 groups are periodic
annotations.note: `rank_eq_zero_iff_isTorsion` states rank 0 ↔ torsion for modules over a domain, applying to ℤ-modules.

annotations.label: Every module is a cokernel of free modules
annotations.note: The tautological presentation exhibits any module via generators and relations, i.e. as a cokernel of free modules.

annotations.label: Monoid object
annotations.note: `MonObj X` is a monoid object in a monoidal category (bundled as `Mon C`).

annotations.label: Torsion-free abelian group
annotations.note: `Monoid.IsTorsionFree` / `AddMonoid.IsTorsionFree` formalizes torsion-freeness.

annotations.label: Unsolvability of the Entscheidungsproblem
annotations.note: Mathlib proves the halting problem is not computable (`halting_problem`), but does not formalize the Entscheidungsproblem for first-order logic or β-normal forms.

annotations.label: Ideal class group is finite
annotations.note: The `Fintype (ClassGroup (𝓞 K))` instance establishes finiteness of the class group of a number field.

annotations.label: Boundary value vs initial value problem
annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes ODE initial-value problems via Picard–Lindelöf but has no concept of boundary-value problems for contrast.

annotations.label: Solvability of polynomial equations by degree
annotations.note: Mathlib has the Abel–Ruffini direction (insolvability for degree ≥ 5) via `solvableByRad` and `AbelRuffini`, but no explicit formula theorems through degree 4.

annotations.label: Markov property (discrete-time)
annotations.note: The discrete-time Markov property is implicit in the Ionescu-Tulcea construction (`condDistrib_trajMeasure` recovers the next-step kernel), but is not stated as a standalone `MarkovProperty` predicate.

annotations.label: Kullback–Leibler divergence of exponentials
annotations.note: General KL divergence is formalized but the closed-form formula `λ₀/λ₁ − 1 − log(λ₀/λ₁)` for two exponentials is not.

annotations.label: Time-homogeneous Markov chain
annotations.note: A time-homogeneous chain corresponds to iterating one kernel `κ` via `traj`, but Mathlib has no explicit `IsTimeHomogeneous` predicate.

annotations.label: Discrete-time Markov chain (DTMC)
annotations.note: Discrete-time iteration of a Markov kernel is constructed via `traj`/`trajMeasure` in Ionescu-Tulcea, but there is no top-level `DTMC` structure.

annotations.label: Discrete-time Markov chain
annotations.note: The law of the DTMC sequence is obtainable via `trajMeasure`, but no dedicated `DTMC` structure is defined.

annotations.label: Signature of a quadratic form
annotations.note: sigPos and sigNeg give the positive/negative parts (p,q) of the signature of a quadratic form.

annotations.label: Signature of a metric
annotations.note: `QuadraticForm.sigPos`/`sigNeg` give the signature for a quadratic form on a vector space, but no `signature` of a metric tensor on a manifold.

annotations.label: Complex conjugate root theorem
annotations.note: `RCLike.aeval_conj` gives `aeval (conj z) p = conj (aeval z p)` for `p : ℝ[X]`, immediately yielding the conjugate-root theorem (used as `simpa [dvd_iff_isRoot, aeval_conj]` in `Analysis/Complex/Polynomial/Basic.lean`).

annotations.label: Real polynomial roots in conjugate pairs
annotations.note: `RCLike.aeval_conj` is the workhorse and is invoked (via `simpa [dvd_iff_isRoot, aeval_conj]`) in `Analysis/Complex/Polynomial/Basic.lean:180` precisely to derive that non-real roots come in conjugate pairs.

annotations.label: Smoothness controls Fourier coefficient decay
annotations.note: The Riemann–Lebesgue lemma gives qualitative decay-to-zero for L¹ functions, but no quantitative `C^k ⇒ |aₙ| = O(n^{-k})` Fourier-coefficient decay statement is formalized.

annotations.label: Rectangle
annotations.note: Mathlib only defines an axis-parallel rectangle as a set in ℂ, not a general quadrilateral with four right angles.

annotations.label: Riemannian metric on a surface
annotations.note: A smoothly varying positive-definite inner product on tangent spaces is formalized as `RiemannianMetric`/`ContMDiffRiemannianMetric`, generalizing the 2×2 surface case.

annotations.label: Metric tensor and length of curves
annotations.note: The metric is `RiemannianMetric` and curve length is `pathELength`, but the index-lowering role of the metric is handled separately by the musical isomorphism.

annotations.label: Second-countable implications
annotations.note: Instances give second-countable ⇒ first-countable, ⇒ separable (to_separableSpace), and ⇒ Lindelöf.

annotations.label: Separable space
annotations.note: SeparableSpace requires a countable dense subset.

annotations.label: Whitney embedding theorem
annotations.note: Mathlib proves a Whitney embedding theorem for compact smooth manifolds of any dimension into Euclidean space.

annotations.label: Orthogonal vectors
annotations.note: Orthogonality of two vectors is `⟪x, y⟫ = 0`; Mathlib has lemmas like `inner_eq_zero_symm` and `Submodule.isOrtho` for sets.

annotations.label: Minimum tree height for n nodes
annotations.note: Only the upper bound `height ≤ numNodes` is provided; no lower bound `height ≥ ⌈log₂(n+1)⌉` is formalized.

annotations.label: Leaves vs. degree-2 nodes relation
annotations.note: `numLeaves = numNodes + 1` is exactly the n0 = n2 + 1 identity under Mathlib's extended-tree convention where every `node` has two children and `nil` is a leaf.

annotations.label: Number of null links
annotations.note: Null links correspond to `nil` leaves in the extended representation, and `numLeaves = numNodes + 1` gives n + 1 null links.

annotations.label: Counting binary trees yields Catalan numbers
annotations.note: `#(treesOfNumNodesEq n) = catalan n` is the precise statement that binary trees with n internal nodes are counted by Catalan numbers.

annotations.label: Absolute Galois group
annotations.note: `absoluteGaloisGroup K` is defined as the automorphism group of the algebraic closure over K.

annotations.label: Buffon's needle approximation of π
annotations.note: Proved in the Archive as `buffon_short`/`buffon_long`, giving the needle-crossing probability `2l/(πd)`.

annotations.label: Cauchy integral theorem
annotations.note: Cauchy's theorem is stated for circular contours via `circleIntegral_eq_zero_of_differentiable_on_off_countable` and `DiffContOnCl.circleIntegral_eq_zero`; the fully general Jordan-curve version is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conjugate subsets
annotations.note: `conjugatesOfSet` collects all conjugates of elements of a set; the conjugate `gSg⁻¹` of a subgroup is given by the pointwise action (`MulAut.conj g • H`), but no dedicated subset-conjugacy relation is defined.

annotations.label: Conjugate subsets
annotations.note: Conjugation of a subgroup as `gHg⁻¹` is realized by the pointwise action `MulAut.conj g • H`, but the binary subset-conjugacy relation is not separately defined.

annotations.label: Covariance as a dot product
annotations.note: `covarianceBilin` formalizes covariance as a continuous (inner-product-like) bilinear form on a Banach space, capturing the dot-product viewpoint.

annotations.label: Set of finite-length sequences of naturals is countable
annotations.note: `denumerableList` makes `List α` denumerable for denumerable α, so `List ℕ` (finite sequences of naturals) is countable.

annotations.label: Adding rationals with common denominator
annotations.note: div_add_div_same states a/c + b/c = (a+b)/c in any division ring.

annotations.label: Hölder's inequality for vector-valued functions
annotations.note: This abstract Hölder estimate for `‖b(f,g)‖` under a bilinear bound generalizes the vector-valued case; see also `MemLp.of_bilin` and `ContinuousLinearMap.holder`.

annotations.label: Eisenstein series as automorphic form
annotations.note: `eisensteinSeriesMF` packages the weight-k Eisenstein series as a `ModularForm` for congruence subgroups.

annotations.label: Separability via dense sequence
annotations.note: `exists_dense_seq` produces a dense sequence in a (nonempty) `SeparableSpace`, capturing the dense-sequence characterization.

annotations.label: Stone–Weierstrass theorem (locally compact spaces)
annotations.note: Mathlib has an `ε`-approximation variant for non-compact X on a compact subset, but the C₀ formulation for locally compact spaces is explicitly listed as future work in the file.

annotations.label: Liouville's transcendence criterion
annotations.note: Mathlib's `exists_pos_real_of_irrational_root` produces the Liouville inequality `1 ≤ (b+1)^d * |α − a/(b+1)| * A` underlying the criterion.

annotations.label: Subgroup of every prime-power order
annotations.note: `exists_subgroup_card_pow_prime` produces a subgroup of order p^n whenever p^n ∣ |G|.

annotations.label: Theorem 1 (proof statement)
annotations.note: Documented in the file as 'Sylow's first theorem', producing a subgroup of order p^n when p^n ∣ |G|.

annotations.label: Constructive existence of Sylow p-subgroup
annotations.note: `exists_subgroup_card_pow_succ` produces K ⊇ H of order p^(n+1) when p^(n+1) ∣ |G| and |H| = p^n.

annotations.label: Entire functions have infinite radius of convergence
annotations.note: `expSeries_radius_eq_top` shows the exp series has radius `∞`; same for `binomialSeries_radius_eq_top_of_nat`.

annotations.label: Exponential of skew-symmetric is orthogonal
annotations.note: exp_mem_unitary_of_mem_skewAdjoint shows the exponential of a skew-adjoint element is unitary; the orthogonal matrix case over ℝ is a special case.

annotations.label: e is transcendental (Lindemann–Weierstrass)
annotations.note: Only the analytical part of Lindemann–Weierstrass (exp_polynomial_approx) is in Mathlib; the transcendence of e itself is not yet proven.

annotations.label: Solutions form a hyperplane (n variables)
annotations.note: Rank–nullity and LinearMap.ker give that a nonzero functional's kernel has codimension one, but the affine zero-set being a hyperplane is not packaged as a statement.

annotations.label: Abelian groups equal Z-modules
annotations.note: The forgetful functor `ModuleCat ℤ → AddCommGrp` is an equivalence, identifying abelian groups with ℤ-modules.

annotations.label: Elements Book II Prop 11
annotations.note: Prop II.11 is the division in extreme and mean ratio; Mathlib defines `goldenRatio` and its defining quadratic but not the geometric construction.

annotations.label: Diagonals of a convex regular pentagon are in golden ratio to sides
annotations.note: The golden ratio constant is defined in `Mathlib.NumberTheory.Real.GoldenRatio`, but its relation to the regular pentagon's diagonal-to-side ratio is not formalized.

annotations.label: Gram–Schmidt procedure
annotations.note: `gramSchmidt` and `gramSchmidtOrthonormalBasis` formalize the Gram–Schmidt process.

annotations.label: QR decomposition
annotations.note: Gram–Schmidt produces an orthonormal basis with an upper-triangular change of basis (the essence of QR), but a matrix QR factorization theorem is not stated.

annotations.label: Halting problem unsolvable
annotations.note: Mathlib proves `halting_problem`: the predicate `(eval c n).Dom` is not a `ComputablePred`.

annotations.label: Maclaurin series of the natural logarithm
annotations.note: `hasSum_pow_div_log_of_abs_lt_one` expresses `log(1-x)` (and via `add` `log(1+x)`) as its Maclaurin series.

annotations.label: Mercator series
annotations.note: The Mercator series `log(1+x) = ∑ (-1)^(n+1) x^n / n` follows from `hasSum_pow_div_log_of_abs_lt_one`.

annotations.label: Hurwitz zeta function
annotations.note: `hurwitzZeta (a : UnitAddCircle) (s : ℂ)` is defined in Mathlib (with `riemannZeta = hurwitzZeta 0`).

annotations.label: Units form abelian multiplicative group
annotations.note: `instCommGroupUnits` makes the units of a `CommMonoid` a `CommGroup`.

annotations.label: Real function defines strict weak order
annotations.note: Pulling back a strict weak order along a function `f` (`f ⁻¹'o r`) is a strict weak order via the `instIsStrictWeakOrder` instance, e.g. `f x < f y` for `f` into ℝ.

annotations.label: Triple integral over R^3
annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the n-dimensional radius/sphere decomposition ∫ f(‖x‖) = c·∫ rⁿ⁻¹ f(r) dr, but not the explicit (r,θ,φ) spherical triple integral.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz inequality (L² case)
annotations.note: The L² Cauchy–Schwarz inequality is the special case p=q=2 of Mathlib's Hölder inequality for Bochner integrals; the abstract Hilbert-space version is `norm_inner_le_norm`.

annotations.label: Hölder's inequality for Lebesgue measure
annotations.note: The Lebesgue-measure case follows by specializing the general Bochner Hölder inequality `integral_mul_norm_le_Lp_mul_Lq` to `volume`.

annotations.label: Hölder's inequality for random variables
annotations.note: The probability/random-variable formulation `E[|XY|] ≤ E[|X|^p]^{1/p}·E[|Y|^q]^{1/q}` is a special case of the general Bochner Hölder when `μ` is a probability measure.

annotations.label: Mean equals variance equals λ
annotations.note: Integration infrastructure exists but neither the mean nor variance of `poissonMeasure` is computed as `λ`.

annotations.label: Expected value
annotations.note: An integration formula against `poissonMeasure` exists, but `E[X] = λ` is not proven.

annotations.label: Variance
annotations.note: Integration infrastructure exists but the variance `λ` of the Poisson measure is not computed.

annotations.label: Improper Riemann integral
annotations.note: Mathlib formalises improper integrals as limits of `intervalIntegral` over expanding intervals (e.g. `intervalIntegral_tendsto_integral_Ioi/Iic`), in the Lebesgue setting.

annotations.label: Bounded polytope
annotations.note: The general notion of a bounded set (contained in a ball) is formalized, but not as a polytope property.

annotations.label: Heine–Borel theorem (lead)
annotations.note: Stated as the Heine–Borel theorem in proper spaces, which covers Euclidean space.

annotations.label: Heine–Borel lemma
annotations.note: Same Heine–Borel statement; the finite-subcover form follows from `isCompact_Icc` plus `isCompact_iff_finite_subcover`.

annotations.label: Compactness in Euclidean space
annotations.note: Heine–Borel characterization in proper spaces (incl. Euclidean space).

annotations.label: Heine–Borel theorem
annotations.note: `isCompact_iff_isClosed_bounded` in T2 proper spaces.

annotations.label: Heine–Borel theorem
annotations.note: Heine–Borel is proved for proper metric spaces (subsuming ℝⁿ): compact iff closed and bounded.

annotations.label: Covariance inequality
annotations.note: The covariance bilinear form is proven positive semidefinite, but the explicit Cauchy–Schwarz bound Cov(X,Y)² ≤ Var(X)·Var(Y) is not stated.

annotations.label: Mantel's theorem
annotations.note: Turán's theorem (which generalizes Mantel's r=2 case) is formalized, but the specific Mantel triangle-free edge-count statement ≤ ⌊n²/4⌋ is not separately given.

annotations.label: p-nilpotency from central Sylow normalizer
annotations.note: `ker_transferSylow_isComplement'` is Burnside's normal p-complement theorem: if N(P) ≤ C(P) then P has a normal complement.

annotations.label: Information theory
annotations.note: The field as such is not a statement, but related infrastructure quantifying information (Kullback–Leibler divergence) is formalized.

annotations.label: Statistical distances
annotations.note: No general 'statistical distance' notion is defined, though specific divergences like klDiv exist as instances.

annotations.label: Relative entropy (Kullback–Leibler divergence)
annotations.note: klDiv μ ν is the Kullback–Leibler divergence (relative entropy) between two measures.

annotations.label: Metrization theorems
annotations.note: Urysohn's metrization theorem (T3 + second-countable ⇒ metrizable) is formalized.

annotations.label: Lie derivative
annotations.note: The Lie bracket of vector fields (`mlieBracket`) is formalized; the general Lie derivative on tensor fields is not, since tensor fields themselves are not formalized.

annotations.label: Classification of vector spaces by dimension
annotations.note: `nonempty_linearEquiv_of_finrank_eq` produces a linear equivalence between two vector spaces with equal finrank.

annotations.label: Pythagorean theorem
annotations.note: The Pythagorean theorem is formalized in inner-product-space angle form.

annotations.label: Earliest statement of the Pythagorean theorem
annotations.note: Same Pythagorean theorem statement is formalized.

annotations.label: First proof of the Pythagorean theorem
annotations.note: The Pythagorean theorem itself is proven in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Proof of the Pythagorean theorem (Nine Chapters)
annotations.note: The Pythagorean theorem is proven in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Statement of the Pythagorean theorem (Sulba Sutras)
annotations.note: The Pythagorean theorem statement is formalized.

annotations.label: Pythagoras and de Gua's theorem cases
annotations.note: The 2-simplex (Pythagorean) case is formalized via the right-angle norm identity, but the 3-simplex de Gua case is not.

annotations.label: Pythagorean theorem (areas of squares)
annotations.note: Mathlib proves the Pythagorean theorem in inner-product/angle form, generalizing the right-triangle square-areas statement.

annotations.label: Length and energy of a path
annotations.note: Path length is formalized as `pathELength` (integral of the speed); the energy functional is not formalized.

annotations.label: Arc length on a parametric surface
annotations.note: Mathlib defines `pathELength` for paths in Riemannian manifolds and `eVariationOn` for arc length in metric spaces, but no surface-specific arc-length integral via a first fundamental form.

annotations.label: Arclength of a parametric curve on a Riemannian manifold
annotations.note: `pathELength I γ a b = ∫⁻ t in Icc a b, ‖mfderiv γ t 1‖ₑ` is exactly the arclength integral on a Riemannian manifold.

annotations.label: Coordinate-independence of length formulas
annotations.note: `pathELength` is defined intrinsically via `mfderiv`, so coordinate-independence holds by construction (no separate change-of-coordinates lemma is needed).

annotations.label: Length independent of parametrization
annotations.note: `pathELength` is proven invariant under monotone (and antitone) reparametrization.

annotations.label: Poisson distribution
annotations.note: `poissonMeasure r` defines the Poisson distribution on ℕ with rate `r`.

annotations.label: Probability of k events
annotations.note: `poissonMeasure_singleton` gives `P{n} = exp(-r) r^n / n!`, the stated PMF.

annotations.label: Probability mass function
annotations.note: The Poisson PMF is given exactly by `poissonMeasure_singleton`.

annotations.label: Hilbert's tenth problem (Matiyasevich)
annotations.note: Mathlib has `pow_dioph` (the power function is Diophantine, a key step in Matiyasevich's theorem) but not the full DPRM resolution / undecidability of Hilbert's tenth problem.

annotations.label: Undecidability of Hilbert's tenth problem
annotations.note: Matiyasevich's key step (the power function is Diophantine) is formalized, but the file's TODO notes the full undecidability/unsolvability conclusion is not yet completed.

annotations.label: Decidability of Presburger arithmetic
annotations.note: Presburger-definable sets are characterized as semilinear (yielding decidability), and `mul_not_definable` captures the addition-but-not-multiplication boundary.

annotations.label: Riemannian geometry
annotations.note: Riemannian distance via path length is formalized; full Riemannian geometry/curvature is not.

annotations.label: Homology
annotations.note: `singularHomologyFunctor` assigns homology objects to a topological space; the general algebraic notion is `HomologicalComplex.homology`.

annotations.label: Functoriality of algebraic topology constructions
annotations.note: Specific constructions (e.g. `singularHomologyFunctor`, the fundamental groupoid functor) are formalized as functors, but no blanket functoriality statement exists.

annotations.label: Induced homomorphism on homology
annotations.note: `singularHomologyFunctor` is functorial in `TopCat`, so a continuous map induces a map on singular homology.

annotations.label: Sedrakyan's inequality
annotations.note: `sq_sum_div_le_sum_sq_div` is documented as Sedrakyan's/Titu's/Engel's lemma: (∑fᵢ)²/∑gᵢ ≤ ∑ fᵢ²/gᵢ.

annotations.label: Conjugation is an involution
annotations.note: The `InvolutiveStar` field `star_involutive : Function.Involutive star` applies to `ℂ` via `Complex.instStarRing` (cf. `star_involutive x := by simp only [eta, neg_neg]` at `Data/Complex/Basic.lean:440`).

annotations.label: Stone–Weierstrass theorem (real numbers)
annotations.note: States that a point-separating subalgebra of `C(X, ℝ)` for compact X is dense, the real Stone-Weierstrass theorem.

annotations.label: Polynomial approximation in two variables
annotations.note: No dedicated bivariate polynomial-approximation lemma, but the result follows from the general real Stone-Weierstrass theorem in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Approximation by sums of products on X×Y
annotations.note: Mathlib has no explicit statement about density of sums of products `g(x)h(y)` in `C(X×Y, ℝ)`, but it is an immediate corollary of the general real Stone-Weierstrass theorem.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz in the plane R²
annotations.note: The R² dot-product case is the two-term instance of the general finite-sum Cauchy–Schwarz `sum_mul_sq_le_sq_mul_sq`.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz in Rⁿ
annotations.note: `sum_mul_sq_le_sq_mul_sq` is exactly (∑fᵢgᵢ)² ≤ (∑fᵢ²)(∑gᵢ²), the Cauchy–Schwarz inequality for the dot product on Rⁿ.

annotations.label: Symplectic group
annotations.note: `symplecticGroup l R` is the submonoid of matrices preserving the standard symplectic form `J`.

annotations.label: Real numbers are Hausdorff
annotations.note: ℝ carries a `MetricSpace`/`MetrizableSpace` structure, so `t2Space_of_metrizableSpace` yields a `T2Space ℝ` instance.

annotations.label: Metric spaces are Hausdorff
annotations.note: Every `MetrizableSpace` (hence every `MetricSpace`) is a `T2Space` via `t2Space_of_metrizableSpace`.

annotations.label: Limit of a binomial distribution
annotations.note: The Poisson limit theorem proves binomial probabilities converge to the Poisson PMF.

annotations.label: Limiting case of binomial
annotations.note: The binomial→Poisson limit as `n→∞` with `np→r` is proved by the Poisson limit theorem.

annotations.label: Poisson limit theorem
annotations.note: This is exactly the formalized Poisson limit theorem (law of rare events).

annotations.label: Convergence via subsequences
annotations.note: `tendsto_of_subseq_tendsto`: if every subsequence has a sub-subsequence tending to `a`, the sequence tends to `a`.

annotations.label: Number of bracketings is Catalan number
annotations.note: The count of binary trees with `n` internal nodes (i.e. bracketings of `n+1` symbols) equals `catalan n`.

annotations.label: Cauchy product
annotations.note: The Cauchy product formula is tsum_mul_tsum_eq_tsum_sum_antidiagonal, matching the antidiagonal-convolution PowerSeries.coeff_mul.

annotations.label: Well-ordering principle
annotations.note: `type_nat_lt` (typeLT ℕ = ω) records that ℕ under `<` is a well order, the content of the well-ordering principle.

annotations.label: Standard order on naturals
annotations.note: `type_nat_lt` states ℕ's standard order is a well order of type ω (ℕ also has a `WellFoundedLT` instance).

annotations.label: Naturals well ordered by ≤
annotations.note: ℕ under its standard order is a well order (type ω), via `type_nat_lt` and the `WellFoundedLT ℕ` instance.

annotations.label: Egyptian approximation for area of a circle
annotations.note: The exact area πr² of a 2D ball is formalized (volume_ball_fin_two), but the specific Egyptian approximation (8d/9)² is not.

annotations.label: Babylonian area of a circle
annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the exact disc area πr², but not the Babylonian approximation of area as one-twelfth the squared circumference.

annotations.label: Nine Chapters geometrical area and volume formulas
annotations.note: Some individual formulas (e.g., the disc area πr²) are formalized via volume measures, but the collected list of area/volume formulas is not a single Mathlib result.

annotations.label: Medians 3:1 ratio
annotations.note: `dist (s.points i) s.centroid = n * dist s.centroid (s.faceOppositeCentroid i)` specializes to the 3:1 ratio at `n = 3`.

annotations.label: Two points determine a line
annotations.note: Mathlib has `affineSpan` of two points but no single canonical statement equivalent to Euclid's `two points determine a line` axiom.

annotations.label: Ring is associative algebra over its center
annotations.note: There is an `instance : Algebra (Subring.center R) R` (and `Ring.toIntAlgebra : Algebra ℤ R`) in `Mathlib.Algebra.Algebra.Basic`.

annotations.label: Noetherian local rings have finite dimension
annotations.note: Under `[CommRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R] [IsLocalRing R]`, an instance provides `FiniteRingKrullDim R`.

annotations.label: Simplicial k-chain
annotations.note: Chain groups of simplicial objects exist via the alternating face map complex, but not formal sums of faces of a geometric simplicial complex.

annotations.label: Vector space of k-chains
annotations.note: The degree-k object of a (singular/simplicial) chain complex realizes this, but not as the free module on geometric k-simplices.

annotations.label: Naturally isomorphic functors
annotations.note: Natural isomorphisms are `Iso` in the functor category; the `NatIso` namespace collects the API including componentwise `IsIso` characterizations.

annotations.label: Convergence iff adherent and Cauchy
annotations.note: Standard uniform-space lemmas state that a Cauchy filter converges iff it has a cluster point.

annotations.label: Dual plane is a projective plane
annotations.note: Mathlib provides `instance : ProjectivePlane (Dual L) (Dual P)`, witnessing that the dual of a projective plane is a projective plane.

annotations.label: Duality in the projective plane
annotations.note: The `ProjectivePlane (Dual L) (Dual P)` instance in Combinatorics/Configuration.lean witnesses point–line duality for projective planes.

annotations.label: Principle of duality (Gergonne)
annotations.note: The duality instance shows that `Dual L`, `Dual P` is again a projective plane, formalizing Gergonne's duality principle.

annotations.label: Contractible implies path connected
annotations.note: Anonymous priority-100 instance at Contractible.lean:108 turns `ContractibleSpace` into `PathConnectedSpace`.

annotations.label: Oriented graph
annotations.note: `Mathlib.Combinatorics.Digraph.Orientation` discusses orientation-forgetting maps, but does not define a standalone `OrientedGraph` predicate.

annotations.label: Discrete spaces and compactness
annotations.note: An infinite discrete space is non-compact: combine `DiscreteTopology` with finiteness arguments; lemmas like `Set.Finite.isCompact` and `discreteTopology_iff_*` cover this. Exact name not verified, treating as partial.

annotations.label: Division ring is semisimple
annotations.note: Instance making a division ring semisimple is present in `SimpleModule.Basic`.

annotations.label: Cycle taken to k-th power
annotations.note: Mathlib characterizes when a power of a cycle is again a cycle (coprimality), but the full gcd-cycle decomposition of a cycle's power is not stated.

annotations.label: Euclidean geometry
annotations.note: Mathlib has the `EuclideanGeometry` namespace built on inner product / affine spaces, which is a model of Euclidean geometry but not the axiomatic 'satisfies all of Euclid's axioms' definition.

annotations.label: Topology from a filter
annotations.note: Mathlib equips `Filter α` with a canonical topology generated by `Set.Iic (𝓟 s)`; this is the topology on the space of filters.

annotations.label: No finite field of size 6
annotations.note: Mathlib's `FieldTheory.Cardinality` characterizes finite field sizes as prime powers, which implies no field of size 6, though the specialization is not stated.

annotations.label: Quantifier elimination over reals
annotations.note: Mathlib has Presburger arithmetic quantifier elimination but not the Tarski–Seidenberg theorem for real-closed fields via CAD.

annotations.label: Function
annotations.note: Functions `α → β` are a primitive notion in Lean's type theory used throughout Mathlib.

annotations.label: Indexed family
annotations.note: Indexed families are represented as ordinary functions `ι → X` in Lean; no separate definition is needed.

annotations.label: Worst case of Euclid's algorithm
annotations.note: Mathlib bounds continued-fraction denominators below by `Nat.fib`, which underlies the worst-case analysis, but does not explicitly state the Lamé worst-case theorem for the Euclidean algorithm.

annotations.label: Tangent via implicit differentiation
annotations.note: Mathlib's implicit function theorem provides the underlying machinery but doesn't state the tangent-slope formula directly.

annotations.label: Schur's lemma
annotations.note: Schur's lemma (endomorphism ring of a simple module is a division ring) is in `SimpleModule.Basic`; categorical version in `CategoryTheory.Preadditive.Schur`.

annotations.label: Functional equation for L-functions
annotations.note: Mathlib has functional equations for Riemann zeta and Dirichlet/Hurwitz L-functions (e.g. in `Mathlib/NumberTheory/LSeries/RiemannZeta.lean`, `AbstractFuncEq.lean`), but not the general Langlands L-function functional equation.

annotations.label: Wedderburn's little theorem
annotations.note: Wedderburn's little theorem is in `Mathlib.RingTheory.LittleWedderburn`.

annotations.label: Transition matrix M
annotations.note: Mathlib defines stochastic matrices in general, but no transition matrix specifically for an iterated game is formalized.

annotations.label: Triple product zero iff linearly dependent
annotations.note: Combining `triple_product_eq_det` with determinant-linear-dependence characterizations gives the result, but it is not stated explicitly for the triple product.

annotations.label: Triple product multilinear and antisymmetric
annotations.note: Multilinearity/alternation follow via `triple_product_eq_det` from determinant properties, but are not stated directly on the triple product.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of inverse
annotations.note: Uniqueness follows from the `Units`/`Group` structure on the matrix ring; the chosen inverse is `A⁻¹` (`nonsing_inv`).

annotations.label: Generalized Stokes theorem (informal)
annotations.note: Only the divergence theorem on rectangles is formalized; no general Stokes theorem for differential forms on manifolds exists.

annotations.label: Stokes' theorem
annotations.note: Only the divergence theorem on rectangles is formalized; the general Stokes theorem `∫_∂Ω ω = ∫_Ω dω` for differential forms is not.

annotations.label: Generalized Stokes theorem
annotations.note: Only the divergence theorem on rectangular boxes in ℝⁿ⁺¹ is formalized; there is no manifold-with-corners ∫_M dω = ∫_{∂M} ω.

annotations.label: Tail-sum formula
annotations.note: Mathlib's layercake formulas (`Mathlib.MeasureTheory.Integral.Layercake`) generalize the tail-sum identity `E[X] = ∫₀^∞ ℙ(X > t) dt` for nonnegative random variables.

annotations.label: Inclusion–exclusion for three events
annotations.note: General finite inclusion–exclusion for measures is available in Mathlib, specializing to three events.

annotations.label: Modular group
annotations.note: The modular group `SL(2, ℤ)` and its quotient are defined in Mathlib's modular forms development.

annotations.label: Exactness from local exactness
annotations.note: The 'local-to-global' exactness criterion is in Mathlib via localization at maximal ideals.

annotations.label: Ring as monoid in Ab
annotations.note: Monoid objects in monoidal categories are formalized; the specific equivalence `Ring ≃ Mon_(Ab)` is not a single named theorem.

annotations.label: Ring object in a category
annotations.note: Monoid objects in monoidal categories exist; an explicit `RingObject`/`Ring_` in a general category isn't packaged.

annotations.label: Maschke semisimplicity
annotations.note: Maschke's theorem is formalized as IsSemisimpleModule k[G] (and IsSemisimpleRing k[G]) for any finite group whose order is invertible, including S_n.

annotations.label: Main theorem on symmetric functions
annotations.note: The fundamental theorem of symmetric polynomials is formalized via the `esymmAlgHom` algebra isomorphism in `Mathlib.RingTheory.MvPolynomial.Symmetric.FundamentalTheorem`.

annotations.label: Root-finding algorithms
annotations.note: Mathlib has Hensel's lemma as a root-finding theorem but no general numerical root-finding algorithm.

annotations.label: Pythagorean theorem for pairwise orthogonal vectors
annotations.note: Mathlib's `OrthogonalFamily` / `Orthonormal.norm_sum_sq`-style lemmas extend Pythagoras to finite orthogonal sums.

annotations.label: Diophantine equations
annotations.note: Mathlib has Pell/Matiyasevic for Diophantine sets and specific Diophantine equations, but no general decision procedure.

annotations.label: Sylvester's law of inertia
annotations.note: Sylvester's law of inertia (uniqueness of the signature) is proved in `Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.QuadraticForm.Signature`.

annotations.label: Sylvester's law of inertia for metric diagonalization
annotations.note: Sylvester's law of inertia is proved for quadratic forms on vector spaces, but not transported to the local diagonalization of a (pseudo-)Riemannian metric on a manifold.

annotations.label: R1 implies R0
annotations.note: An anonymous priority-100 `R0Space X` instance is given for `[R1Space X]` at `Mathlib/Topology/Separation/Basic.lean:889`.

annotations.label: Inverse Fourier transform formula
annotations.note: Real.fourierIntegralInv is the integral defining the inverse Fourier transform.

annotations.label: Infinite series for the logarithm
annotations.note: The Mercator/Newton power series for the logarithm is formalized.

annotations.label: Harmonic mean
annotations.note: The harmonic mean appears informally in the HM-GM inequality discussion but is not given a top-level definition.

annotations.label: Haar measure on the reals is Lebesgue measure
annotations.note: Lebesgue `volume` on ℝ is an additive Haar measure, established in `MeasureTheory.Measure.Lebesgue.EqHaar`.

annotations.label: Upper bound via Halmos argument
annotations.note: The inequality `1 - x ≤ exp(-x)` exists in Mathlib but the Halmos-style birthday bound is not assembled there.

annotations.label: Regular implies R1
annotations.note: Priority-100 `R1Space X` instance under `[RegularSpace X]` at `Mathlib/Topology/Separation/Regular.lean:165` (with comment `A regular space is R₁`).

annotations.label: Dual of C(K) as Radon measures
annotations.note: Riesz–Markov–Kakutani represents (positive/real) functionals by measures, but C(K)* ≅ Radon measures is not packaged as a normed dual isomorphism.

annotations.label: Rotational symmetry
annotations.note: Mathlib has `Rotation` in oriented Euclidean geometry but no predicate stating an object has rotational symmetry.

annotations.label: Sequentially complete topological group
annotations.note: `SequentiallyComplete` exists as a general uniform-space notion in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Forgetting-indices map is bijective iff pairwise disjoint
annotations.note: Lemmas like the sigma-to-iUnion projection being injective under pairwise disjointness exist (e.g. via `Set.PairwiseDisjoint`), but I could not locate a single theorem stating the equivalence verbatim.

annotations.label: Bimodule
annotations.note: Bimodules have no dedicated class but are expressed as `[Module R M] [Module Sᵐᵒᵖ M] [SMulCommClass R Sᵐᵒᵖ M]`, as documented in this file.

annotations.label: Radicals of primary decomposition form the assassinator
annotations.note: Lasker.lean shows the radicals of a minimal primary decomposition equal `N.associatedPrimes` (the assassinator).

annotations.label: Almost symplectic manifold / symplectic form
annotations.note: Only the linear-algebra notion of a symplectic (nondegenerate alternating) form is present via SymplecticGroup; no smoothly-varying form on a manifold's tangent bundle is defined.

annotations.label: Lattice of topologies on a set
annotations.note: Mathlib provides `instance : CompleteLattice (TopologicalSpace α)` with `⊥` discrete and `⊤` indiscrete.

annotations.label: Algorithm (effective method)
annotations.note: The Church–Turing notion of effective method is captured by Mathlib's Turing machines and partial recursive functions, but no direct definition of 'algorithm' exists.

annotations.label: Lattice arrangement
annotations.note: Mathlib defines `ZLattice` (a discrete cocompact ℤ-submodule of a real vector space) which underlies lattice packings, but does not formalize lattice packings themselves.

annotations.label: FOIL method for binomial multiplication
annotations.note: FOIL `(a+b)(c+d) = ac+ad+bc+bd` is an immediate consequence of `mul_add`/`add_mul`; not bundled as a separate named lemma but routinely closed by `ring`.

annotations.label: Special functions analytic on suitable domains
annotations.note: Specific special functions (zeta, Gamma, hurwitzZeta, etc.) are shown analytic on their domains, but there is no overarching statement.

annotations.label: Cantor space and Baire space
annotations.note: Mathlib has Cantor space as ℕ → Bool / ℕ → Fin 2 via the standard product topology, but no first-class `BaireSpace = ℕ → ℕ` definition tied to descriptive set theory.

annotations.label: Dimension equals transcendence degree
annotations.note: Noether normalization is formalized as the key tool, but the explicit Krull-dimension = transcendence-degree theorem is only flagged as a TODO.

annotations.label: Three or more terms in proportion; geometric progressions (Def. 8)
annotations.note: Geometric progressions exist in Mathlib (e.g., `geom_series`, `geom_sum_eq`), but Euclid's Definition 8 itself is not formalized.

annotations.label: Galois group of finite field extension is cyclic
annotations.note: An anonymous instance `IsCyclic Gal(L/K)` for finite `L` is given in `Mathlib/FieldTheory/Finite/Basic.lean`.

annotations.label: Length of cross product is parallelogram area
annotations.note: The identity `‖a ⨯₃ b‖ = ‖a‖ * ‖b‖ * sin (angle a b)` is formalized, but Mathlib does not separately define the parallelogram's area to equate with it.

annotations.label: Regular function on an algebraic set
annotations.note: Regular functions appear scheme-theoretically as structure-sheaf sections; the classical restriction-to-V definition is not named.

annotations.label: Coordinate system
annotations.note: Mathlib has linear-algebraic `Basis` and manifold-theoretic `ChartedSpace`, but no general abstract definition of `coordinate system`.

annotations.label: Spanning set and basis
annotations.note: A basis is `Basis`; spanning means `Submodule.span R s = ⊤`.

annotations.label: Multiplication determined by basis
annotations.note: Bilinearity-from-basis is available via `Basis.constr` / `TensorProduct.lift`, but no dedicated lemma about determining algebra multiplication by its action on basis elements.

annotations.label: Dual set
annotations.note: Mathlib constructs the dual family via `Basis.dualBasis` (and `Basis.coord`) for a basis, but no general 'dual set' for an arbitrary independent family is defined.

annotations.label: Dual set is a basis when finite-dimensional
annotations.note: `Basis.dualBasis` is a `Basis ι R (Dual R M)` for a finite basis `b`.

annotations.label: Dual basis (finite-dimensional)
annotations.note: `Basis.dualBasis` is exactly the dual basis associated to a finite basis.

annotations.label: Infinite-dimensional dual: independent but not a basis
annotations.note: `Basis.dualBasis` functionals are linearly independent, and `lift_rank_lt_rank_dual` shows they cannot span in infinite dimension, but the combined 'not a basis' claim is not a single lemma.

annotations.label: Dual identified with function space on a basis
annotations.note: Coordinate functionals `Basis.coord`/`dualBasis` exist, but no equivalence `Dual R (ι →₀ R) ≃ (ι → R)` for the infinite-dimensional identification was found.

annotations.label: Dual basis
annotations.note: `Basis.dualBasis` is the dual basis of the dual space.

annotations.label: Tensor equality componentwise
annotations.note: `Basis.ext_elem_iff` states two module elements are equal iff all their basis coordinates agree.

annotations.label: Basis between independent and spanning sets
annotations.note: `Basis.extend`/`Basis.extendLe` extend a linearly independent set to a basis contained in a spanning set.

annotations.label: Extension of kernel basis to full basis
annotations.note: Extends a linearly independent set (e.g., a kernel basis) to a basis of the whole space.

annotations.label: Coordinate vector / column matrix
annotations.note: The coordinate vector of x is `b.repr x` (or `b.equivFun x`).

annotations.label: Component of a tensor
annotations.note: A component as the coefficient of a basis element is captured by the coordinate map `Basis.repr`, but Mathlib has no dedicated 'tensor component' notion.

annotations.label: Coordinates on a basis
annotations.note: `Basis.repr` is the linear equivalence sending `v` to its coordinates `ι →₀ R`.

annotations.label: Coordinate map is an isomorphism
annotations.note: `Basis.repr` is by definition a `LinearEquiv` between `M` and `ι →₀ R`, hence an isomorphism.

annotations.label: Tensor product from bases
annotations.note: `Basis.tensorProduct` builds a basis of `M ⊗ N` from bases of `M` and `N`.

annotations.label: Tensor product of vectors via basis decomposition
annotations.note: `Basis.tensorProduct_apply` describes how `v ⊗ w` expands in the product basis.

annotations.label: CW complex
annotations.note: Classical (relative) CW complexes are formalized via `RelCWComplex`/`CWComplex`.

annotations.label: Composition of morphisms in a category
annotations.note: `Category.assoc` is a field of the `Category` typeclass asserting `(f ≫ g) ≫ h = f ≫ (g ≫ h)`.

annotations.label: Epi-mono factorization
annotations.note: `Abelian.imageFactorisation` gives the canonical epi–mono image factorisation in any abelian category.

annotations.label: Localization
annotations.note: `Localization W` constructs the category where morphisms in `W` become isomorphisms, with the `IsLocalization` predicate characterizing it.

annotations.label: Tensor-hom adjunction
annotations.note: `ihom.adjunction` gives `(A ⊗ -) ⊣ ihom A` in any monoidal closed category; abelian groups (ℤ-modules) are the special case via the `ModuleCat` monoidal closed instance.

annotations.label: Currying in cartesian closed category
annotations.note: `ihom.adjunction` with `MonoidalClosed.curry`/`uncurry` gives the currying adjunction; cartesian closed categories are the cartesian-monoidal special case (`CartesianClosed`).

annotations.label: Disjoint union is the coproduct in Set
annotations.note: Category theory in Mathlib has coproduct constructions in `Type`/`Set`, but I did not locate a single named theorem matching the article's wording.

annotations.label: Surjections are epimorphisms in Set
annotations.note: epi_iff_surjective proves a morphism in Type is an epimorphism iff it is surjective.

annotations.label: Bijections are isomorphisms in Set
annotations.note: States `IsIso f ↔ Function.Bijective f` in the category `Type`.

annotations.label: Correspondence with mono/epi/iso
annotations.note: Types.mono_iff_injective, Types.epi_iff_surjective, and Types.isIso_iff_bijective establish the correspondence in the category of types.

annotations.label: Tensor product decomposition of Cl(U+V)
annotations.note: equivProd gives CliffordAlgebra (Q₁.prod Q₂) ≅ the graded tensor product of the two Clifford algebras.

annotations.label: T1 but not Hausdorff: cofinite/cocountable
annotations.note: `CofiniteTopology X` has a `T1Space` instance and an `IrreducibleSpace` instance for infinite `X` (which precludes T₂), matching this example.

annotations.label: Universal property of completion
annotations.note: Mathlib provides `Completion.extension f` extending any uniformly continuous function to the completion, and `Completion.extension_unique` proving uniqueness.

annotations.label: Distance on Cauchy sequences is a pseudometric
annotations.note: Mathlib first equips the completion with a `PseudoMetricSpace` structure, then derives a `MetricSpace` via the separation quotient.

annotations.label: Bounded operators B(H)
annotations.note: An instance gives `CStarAlgebra (E →L[ℂ] E)` for a Hilbert space `E`, i.e. `B(H)` is a C⋆-algebra.

annotations.label: Bounded bijection is an isomorphism
annotations.note: A bijective bounded linear map between Banach spaces has a bounded inverse (ContinuousLinearEquiv).

annotations.label: Evaluation map is a ring homomorphism
annotations.note: Evaluation at a point is a ring homomorphism `C(X,R) → R` via `ContinuousMap.evalRingHom` (or `Pi.evalRingHom` analogues).

annotations.label: Ring of continuous functions
annotations.note: `CommRing C(α, β)` gives the ring of continuous maps; taking `β = ℂ` yields complex-valued continuous functions.

annotations.label: Continuous real-valued functions
annotations.note: `C(X, ℝ)` is a commutative ring via the general `ContinuousMap` ring instance.

annotations.label: Lattice in C(X,R)
annotations.note: The lattice structure on `C(α, β)` (with sup/inf inherited pointwise) is provided; sub-lattice membership is then standard `Set` notion.

annotations.label: Inscribed angle theorem for circles
annotations.note: Mathlib4 proves the oriented-angle inscribed-angle/cyclic-quadrilateral identity for cospherical points (`Cospherical.two_zsmul_oangle_eq`), giving one direction of the inscribed-angle theorem.

annotations.label: Cauchy-continuous extension criterion
annotations.note: The Cauchy-continuous extension lemma per se is absent; Mathlib instead provides `uniformContinuous_uniformly_extend` / `Dense.extend` for the uniformly-continuous version.

annotations.label: Negative exponents in a monoid
annotations.note: `DivInvMonoid.zpow` defines integer powers for invertible elements.

annotations.label: Negative integer exponent in a monoid
annotations.note: `DivInvMonoid.zpow` extends to negative integer exponents.

annotations.label: Negative powers
annotations.note: `zpow` defines integer (including negative) powers for invertible elements in a `DivInvMonoid`/`Group`.

annotations.label: Order on empty set
annotations.note: `instance : LinearOrder Empty := .ofSubsingleton` provides the (necessarily unique) linear order on the empty type.

annotations.label: A_n simple for n ≥ 5
annotations.note: alternatingGroup.isSimpleGroup proves A_n is simple when 5 ≤ card α.

annotations.label: Normal subgroups of finite S_n
annotations.note: A_n is proved normal in S_n, but the full classification of all normal subgroups of finite S_n is not formalized.

annotations.label: Inverse via reversing cycles
annotations.note: `formPerm_reverse` shows reversing a cycle yields the inverse permutation.

annotations.label: Superadditivity of limit inferior
annotations.note: `Filter.le_liminf_add` proves `liminf u + liminf v ≤ liminf (u + v)`.

annotations.label: Subadditivity of limit superior
annotations.note: `Filter.limsup_add_le` proves `limsup (u + v) ≤ limsup u + limsup v`.

annotations.label: Multiplicative inequalities for non-negative sequences
annotations.note: `Filter.limsup_mul_le`, `Filter.le_liminf_mul`, and `Filter.liminf_mul_le` provide the multiplicative analogues for non-negative functions.

annotations.label: Alphabet dictionary order
annotations.note: Finite linear orders (and `List.Lex` dictionary orders) are formalized, but there is no specific 'alphabet' linear order.

annotations.label: Finite total order is a well order
annotations.note: `Fintype.to_wellFoundedLT` gives `WellFoundedLT` for any finite preorder, hence finite linear orders are well orders.

annotations.label: Stone space is compact; isolated types
annotations.note: The CompactSpace instance on CompleteType T α is proved (the isolated-points correspondence is not separately formalized).

annotations.label: Iteration of a function
annotations.note: `Function.iterate f n` (notation `f^[n]`) is the standard iteration of a self-map.

annotations.label: Group structure on fundamental group
annotations.note: A `Group` instance on `FundamentalGroup X x` is provided via `End` of the fundamental groupoid.

annotations.label: Simplicial map
annotations.note: `PreAbstractSimplicialComplex.map` pushes faces forward along a vertex map, but a bundled simplicial-map type is not defined.

annotations.label: Poisson kernel formula on the disk
annotations.note: Mathlib has the Poisson integral formula on disks via `poissonKernel` and `HarmonicContOnCl.circleAverage_poissonKernel_smul`.

annotations.label: Poisson integral formula
annotations.note: The 2D disk version of the Poisson integral formula is formalized via the Poisson kernel; higher-dimensional ball versions are not.

annotations.label: Principle of superposition
annotations.note: Closure of harmonic functions under sums and scalar multiplication is given by `HarmonicOnNhd.add`, `HarmonicAt.add`, and `HarmonicAt.const_smul`.

annotations.label: Quasi-isomorphism
annotations.note: `QuasiIso f` holds when `f` induces isomorphisms on homology in all degrees.

annotations.label: Quotient ring
annotations.note: `R ⧸ I` is the quotient ring with `Ideal.Quotient.mk` as the projection.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic of a bounded finite poset
annotations.note: Defined as `mu 𝕜 (⊥ : α) ⊤` for a finite bounded order, matching the Möbius-function definition described in the article.

annotations.label: Mean value property
annotations.note: Circle-average mean value property is proved for `f : ℂ → ℝ` (with a `HarmonicContOnCl` variant); no ball/volume version or higher-dimensional version found.

annotations.label: Even integers countably infinite
annotations.note: ℤ is denumerable in Mathlib, but the specific bijection ℕ → {even integers} via n ↦ 2n isn't named.

annotations.label: Completeness is a metric property, not topological
annotations.note: Mathlib distinguishes `CompleteSpace` (a property of the uniform structure) from `IsCompletelyMetrizableSpace` (a topological property: existence of some complete compatible metric). This distinction embodies the fact that completeness depe

annotations.label: Completely metrizable space
annotations.note: Mathlib defines `IsCompletelyMetrizableSpace X` as a typeclass asserting that there exists a `MetricSpace` structure compatible with the topology making the space complete.

annotations.label: Definition of immersion
annotations.note: `IsImmersion I J n f` formalizes the notion of an immersion (injective differential at every point) between smooth manifolds.

annotations.label: Immersion, embedding and submersion
annotations.note: `IsImmersion`/`IsImmersionAt` are formalized in `Manifold.Immersion`; submersions and smooth embeddings (beyond `Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.SmoothEmbedding`) are not fully present as named definitions.

annotations.label: Immersion and immersed submanifold
annotations.note: `IsImmersion I J n f` captures `f` having injective differential at every point of a smooth manifold.

annotations.label: Classification of 2-transitive permutation groups
annotations.note: Multiple-transitivity infrastructure (IsMultiplyPretransitive, IsPreprimitive) is defined, but the CFSG-dependent classification of 2-transitive groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Commutative simple rings are fields
annotations.note: Mathlib has the equivalence between commutative simple rings and fields in `SimpleRing.Basic`.

annotations.label: Basis for finite products
annotations.note: IsTopologicalBasis.prod shows products of basis elements form a basis (binary; isTopologicalBasis_pi for the indexed case).

annotations.label: Mazur–Ulam theorem for isometries
annotations.note: The Mazur–Ulam theorem is in `Mathlib.Analysis.Normed.Affine.MazurUlam`, asserting surjective isometries between normed real spaces are affine.

annotations.label: Well-formed word
annotations.note: Well-formedness is encoded as membership in the language via `Membership (List α) (Language α)`; no separate `WellFormed` predicate exists.

annotations.label: Adjoint representation via tensor product
annotations.note: Mathlib has Lie-module structure on tensor products in `Algebra.Lie.TensorProduct`, capturing the adjoint action on `V ⊗ V*`.

annotations.label: Self-adjoint operator
annotations.note: `LinearPMap.IsSelfAdjoint A ↔ A† = A` is the self-adjointness of a densely-defined unbounded operator.

annotations.label: Local diffeomorphism and inverse function theorem
annotations.note: The direction local-diffeomorphism ⇒ `mfderiv` is a linear iso is proved, but the converse (manifold inverse function theorem: invertible differential ⇒ local diffeomorphism) is still a TODO.

annotations.label: Dot product
annotations.note: `Matrix.dotProduct v w = ∑ i, v i * w i` is exactly the dot product, generalized to any ring and index type.

annotations.label: Dot product as matrix multiplication
annotations.note: `Matrix.dotProduct v w = ∑ i, v i * w i` is defined and `Matrix.mul_apply` ties it back to the 1×1 matrix entry of the product.

annotations.label: Dot product as matrix product
annotations.note: `Matrix.dotProduct` plus `Matrix.mul_apply` yield this identification, but no single lemma states the dot product equals the unique entry of `xᵀ y`.

annotations.label: Radial speed
annotations.note: The dot product underlying radial speed is formalized (`Matrix.dotProduct`/inner product), but the physics statement is not.

annotations.label: Gaussian elimination
annotations.note: Mathlib has no general row-reduction algorithm, but proves over a field every matrix factors as transvections·diagonal·transvections (a Gauss-pivot variant).

annotations.label: Row reduction as matrix decomposition
annotations.note: Mathlib provides a related decomposition M = L·D·L' with L, L' products of transvections and D diagonal, rather than the precise LU/PLU statement.

annotations.label: Row reduction (Gaussian elimination)
annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes reduction of a matrix to diagonal form by transvections, the algebraic core of Gaussian elimination, but not the augmented-matrix solving procedure.

annotations.label: Matrix multiplication
annotations.note: The heterogeneous matrix multiplication instance `HMul (Matrix l m α) (Matrix m n α) (Matrix l n α)` is defined directly in `Mathlib/Data/Matrix/Mul.lean`.

annotations.label: Matrix product (m×n times n×p)
annotations.note: The `HMul` instance and `Matrix.mul_apply` give exactly the m×p product matrix of an m×n and n×p matrix.

annotations.label: Existence condition for matrix product
annotations.note: The dimension-matching condition is enforced at the type level via the `HMul (Matrix l m α) (Matrix m n α)` instance rather than stated as a proposition.

annotations.label: Non-commutativity of matrix multiplication
annotations.note: Mathlib's `Matrix.instRing` deliberately does not provide commutativity for `n ≥ 2`, but there is no explicit `¬ Commute A B` example as a named lemma.

annotations.label: Product of two matrices
annotations.note: Matrix multiplication is defined via the `HMul`/`Mul` instances on `Matrix m n α` in `Mathlib.Data.Matrix.Mul`.

annotations.label: Lebesgue integral via improper Riemann integral
annotations.note: Mathlib does not define the Lebesgue integral as an improper Riemann integral, but `IntegralEqImproper` proves the resulting equality for integrable functions.

annotations.label: Prokhorov's theorem
annotations.note: Multiple versions of Prokhorov's theorem are formalized in `Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Measure/Prokhorov.lean`.

annotations.label: Relationship between left and right Haar measures via inversion
annotations.note: Inverting a left-invariant measure yields a right-invariant measure (`Measure.inv`).

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz inequality
annotations.note: The `p = q = 2` case of `lintegral_mul_le_Lp_mul_Lq`, together with `L2Space.lean` lemmas, gives the integral Cauchy–Schwarz inequality.

annotations.label: Poles of a meromorphic function
annotations.note: Poles are captured by `MeromorphicAt.order < 0` (the function tends to infinity there), but Mathlib has no dedicated named 'pole' predicate.

annotations.label: Tensor product is a bifunctor
annotations.note: The monoidal-category structure on `ModuleCat R` encodes the bifunctorial behavior of tensor product.

annotations.label: Exponentiation
annotations.note: Monoid.npow (with HPow notation ^) defines raising a base to a natural-number power.

annotations.label: Exponentiation (positive integer exponent)
annotations.note: `Monoid.npow` is the canonical inductive definition of iterated multiplication on a monoid.

annotations.label: Inductive definition of positive integer exponentiation
annotations.note: `Monoid.npow_zero` and `Monoid.npow_succ` provide the inductive base and step.

annotations.label: Exponentiation in a monoid
annotations.note: `Monoid.npow` is monoid exponentiation.

annotations.label: Matrix power
annotations.note: Square matrices form a monoid, so `A^n` is given by `Monoid.npow`; many `Matrix.*_pow` lemmas exist.
