annotations.label: Nonnegative integer powers
annotations.note: `Monoid.npow` (exposed via `HPow`/`^`) defines nonnegative integer powers with `npow_zero` and `npow_succ`.

annotations.label: Nonnegative integer powers
annotations.note: `Monoid.npow`/`HPow` provide ℕ-indexed powers in a monoid/ring.

annotations.label: Dirichlet characters completely multiplicative
annotations.note: `DirichletCharacter R n := MulChar (ZMod n) R`, and `MulChar.map_mul` gives `χ(ab)=χ(a)χ(b)` for all arguments.

annotations.label: Naturals under divisibility
annotations.note: Mathlib has `Nat.gcd`/`Nat.lcm` as a `GCDMonoid`, and `GCDMonoid` gives a lattice on associates, but there is no direct `Lattice ℕ` instance using divisibility as the order.

annotations.label: Naturals under gcd/lcm
annotations.note: `GCDMonoid ℕ` provides `gcd`/`lcm`; the comment in `Mathlib.Algebra.GCDMonoid.Basic` notes gcd/lcm form a lattice on associates but no direct `Lattice ℕ`-by-divisibility instance is provided.

annotations.label: Natural numbers form an infinite set
annotations.note: Mathlib provides an `Infinite ℕ` instance, equivalent to `(Set.univ : Set ℕ).Infinite`.

annotations.label: Peano axiom 5 (induction)
annotations.note: Nat.rec is the core induction/recursion principle for ℕ.

annotations.label: Mathematical induction
annotations.note: The principle of mathematical induction on ℕ is built into Lean as Nat.rec; Mathlib's Nat.le_induction in Mathlib.Data.Nat.Init packages the usual 'base + step' form.

annotations.label: Principle of mathematical induction
annotations.note: The induction principle for ℕ is the core recursor `Nat.rec`, available throughout Mathlib.

annotations.label: Peano axioms
annotations.note: `Nat` satisfies the Peano axioms (zero, injective successor, recursor `Nat.rec`), but Mathlib has no single bundled `PeanoAxioms` structure.

annotations.label: Axiom of induction
annotations.note: The induction/recursion principle `Nat.rec` is the core recursor, with variants like `Nat.le_induction` and `Nat.strong_induction_on` in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Proofs by mathematical induction
annotations.note: Mathematical induction is the recursor Nat.rec, generated automatically with the natural numbers (Lean core).

annotations.label: Hausdorff distance is a metric on compact subsets
annotations.note: `NonemptyCompacts.instMetricSpace` endows nonempty compact subsets with the Hausdorff metric.

annotations.label: Set exponentiation
annotations.note: `E^F` is represented by the function type `F → E` in Lean.

annotations.label: Product of connected spaces is connected
annotations.note: Instances at Basic.lean:710,714 prove `ConnectedSpace (∀ i, X i)` and `ConnectedSpace (α × β)`.

annotations.label: Tangent bundle via derivations
annotations.note: `Mathlib.Geometry.Manifold.DerivationBundle` constructs pointwise derivations of the smooth function algebra and relates them to the tangent space, but the identification of the tangent bundle with global derivations is not stated as a theo

annotations.label: Polynomial GCD in simplifier
annotations.note: Polynomial GCD is defined in Mathlib via EuclideanDomain/Polynomial.gcd, though it is not used for fraction simplification.

annotations.label: Properties of polynomial rings
annotations.note: `Polynomial R` inherits `IsDomain` from `R`.

annotations.label: Product of two polynomials
annotations.note: `Polynomial` carries a `Semiring`/`CommRing` instance and `Polynomial.mul_def` defines polynomial multiplication via finitely supported convolution of coefficients.

annotations.label: Abel–Ruffini theorem
annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes one direction (solvable-by-radicals ⇒ solvable Galois group); the explicit `Equation.quintic` example/witness needed for the full Abel–Ruffini statement is not present.

annotations.label: Median of uniform distribution
annotations.note: Uniform distributions exist via `IsUniform`, but the median (midpoint) is not formalized as Mathlib has no median.

annotations.label: Random selection / simple random sample
annotations.note: The uniform distribution over a set (IsUniform / PMF.uniformOfFinset) captures equal-probability random selection but not the survey-sampling notion of a simple random sample.

annotations.label: Ordered pairs of numbers
annotations.note: Mathlib supplies `Module` instances on products like `ℝ × ℝ` via `Prod.module`.

annotations.label: Pseudometric spaces are preregular not Hausdorff
annotations.note: Pseudometrizable spaces are `RegularSpace`, hence `R1Space` (preregular) by the `RegularSpace → R1Space` instance; the non-T₀ (non-Hausdorff) aspect is not a separate lemma.

annotations.label: Distance via scalar part of quaternion
annotations.note: The scalar part `Quaternion.re` exists, but the elliptic distance formula in terms of it is not formalized.

annotations.label: Preregular spaces are R0
annotations.note: An anonymous instance `[R1Space X] : R0Space X` (Basic.lean:889) shows preregular spaces are R₀.

annotations.label: Reduction of ratios
annotations.note: Rational numbers are stored in reduced form via `Rat`, but ratio-reduction as a named operation is not a separate Mathlib concept.

annotations.label: Continuous-time Fourier transform formula
annotations.note: Real.fourierIntegral is the integral defining the continuous Fourier transform on ℝ.

annotations.label: Momentum space fields
annotations.note: Mathlib has a general Fourier transform (Real.fourierIntegral), but its application to quantum field operators to obtain momentum-space fields is not formalized.

annotations.label: Real numbers and Euclidean space as topological groups
annotations.note: Mathlib has instances making `ℝ` and `ℝⁿ` topological additive groups.

annotations.label: Order on the number line
annotations.note: The linear order on ℝ encodes the number-line order; `lt_trichotomy` and `LinearOrder` formalize the informal left/right comparison.

annotations.label: Neighbourhood system on the real line
annotations.note: The standard topology on `ℝ` is provided as a `TopologicalSpace` instance via its metric/uniform structure; the neighbourhood-system description is not the primary form.

annotations.label: Standard topology on the real line
annotations.note: `ℝ` carries the standard topology coming from its metric instance.

annotations.label: Half-line [0,∞) not compact
annotations.note: Real line is shown non-compact via `RealNormedSpace.noncompactSpace`; the specific statement for `[0,∞)` is not a named lemma but is a direct consequence.

annotations.label: Real line not compact
annotations.note: Mathlib has a `NoncompactSpace ℝ` instance.

annotations.label: Tensor product of representations / algebras
annotations.note: Tensor products of representations exist in `RepresentationTheory`, but Littlewood–Richardson decomposition is not formalized.

annotations.label: Finite chains have max and min
annotations.note: `Set.Finite.exists_max_image` (and `Finset.exists_max_image`) yield a max element of a nonempty finite set in a linear order.

annotations.label: Complete bipartite graph
annotations.note: `SimpleGraph.completeBipartiteGraph V W` on `V ⊕ W` adjoins every cross-side pair.

annotations.label: Joint distribution of a sequence
annotations.note: ℝ^∞ is standard Borel via the pi instance, but standardness of the joint-distribution probability space is not formalized.

annotations.label: Product of countably many standard spaces
annotations.note: StandardBorelSpace.pi gives closure under countable products of standard Borel spaces; the probability-space statement is not separately formalized.

annotations.label: Complex unital *-algebra generated
annotations.note: `StarSubalgebra.adjoin` (and `StarAlgebra.adjoin`) defines the unital *-algebra generated by a set.

annotations.label: Real line as subalgebra of complex numbers
annotations.note: The general infrastructure exists (`algebraMap ℝ ℂ`, `Subalgebra.range`/`Algebra.bot`) but the specific `ℝ ⊆ ℂ` subalgebra example isn't a named decl.

annotations.label: Subgroup is a topological group
annotations.note: Mathlib has `instance (S : Subgroup G) : IsTopologicalGroup S`.

annotations.label: Quotient vector space
annotations.note: The quotient `M ⧸ S` of a module by a submodule (and its `Module` structure) is built via `Submodule.quotientRel` in `LinearAlgebra/Quotient/Defs.lean`.

annotations.label: Tensor contraction
annotations.note: Contraction of a vector against a dual vector is `TensorProduct.contractLeft`/`contractRight`.

annotations.label: D(U) is complete LCTVS with Heine–Borel property
annotations.note: The locally convex TVS structure on `TestFunction` is established, but completeness and the Heine–Borel (Montel) property are not proved.

annotations.label: Hausdorff distance is a metric on compact subsets
annotations.note: NonemptyCompacts.instMetricSpace gives a MetricSpace structure on nonempty compact subsets whose dist is the Hausdorff distance (NonemptyCompacts.dist_eq).

annotations.label: Compact metric space is second-countable, separable, Lindelöf
annotations.note: Compact metric spaces have instances making them second-countable / separable (e.g., `secondCountable_of_proper`); individual lemmas exist but a single combined statement is not found.

annotations.label: Turing completeness
annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes that Turing machine models compute exactly the partial recursive functions, but not that the Game of Life is Turing complete.

annotations.label: Invertible elements of Banach algebra
annotations.note: Mathlib gives topological group structure on `Mˣ` for topological monoids satisfying continuity hypotheses; the Banach-algebra case is an instance of this.

annotations.label: Structure space of Banach algebra compact
annotations.note: `CompactSpace (characterSpace 𝕜 A)` instance for proper `𝕜` is in `Mathlib/Analysis/Normed/Algebra/Basic.lean`.

annotations.label: Quadratic reciprocity via floor
annotations.note: `ZMod.quadratic_reciprocity` (and `ZMod.eisenstein_lemma`) formalize quadratic reciprocity via the Eisenstein floor-sum method.

annotations.label: Composition of differential operators
annotations.note: Mathlib has chain-rule composition (`deriv.comp`, `fderiv.comp`) but does not formalize composition as an algebra operation on differential operators.

annotations.label: Length of a segment
annotations.note: There is no separate `length` of `affineSegment`, but `dist P Q` plays this role; no dedicated definition was found.

annotations.label: Every vector space has a basis
annotations.note: `exists_basis : ∃ s : Set V, Nonempty (Basis s K V)` proves every vector space has a basis.

annotations.label: Hölder generalization
annotations.note: `inner_le_Lp_mul_Lq` is Hölder's inequality ∑fᵢgᵢ ≤ (∑fᵢ^p)^(1/p)(∑gᵢ^q)^(1/q), generalizing Cauchy–Schwarz.

annotations.label: Galois theory
annotations.note: Galois theory is formalized via `IsGalois` and the fundamental theorem `intermediateFieldEquivSubgroup` giving the Galois correspondence.

annotations.label: Unit interval is compact
annotations.note: Every `Icc a b` (in particular `[0,1]`) is compact via the `CompactIccSpace` instance.

annotations.label: [0,1] compact, (0,1) not, rationals in [0,1] not
annotations.note: `isCompact_Icc` covers `[0,1]`; the open interval and rationals subset are noncompact via `RealNormedSpace.noncompactSpace` and basic arguments.

annotations.label: Closed intervals in R are compact
annotations.note: isCompact_Icc proves Icc compact for any conditionally complete linear order with order topology, ℝ included.

annotations.label: Closed interval is compact
annotations.note: `isCompact_Icc` (exported from `CompactIccSpace`) shows closed intervals in linearly-ordered topological spaces are compact.

annotations.label: Completeness iff closed bounded sets compact
annotations.note: One direction (closed intervals in a conditionally complete order are compact) is `isCompact_Icc`, but the full biconditional characterization is not formalized.

annotations.label: Closed disks and circles compact; open disk not
annotations.note: Closed balls in proper spaces are compact (`isCompact_closedBall`) and spheres too (`isCompact_sphere`); open balls in normed spaces aren't compact via `RealNormedSpace.noncompactSpace`.

annotations.label: Convention lcm(a,0)=0
annotations.note: lcm_zero_right/lcm_zero_left (and Nat.lcm_zero_right) encode exactly the convention lcm(a,0)=0=lcm(0,a).

annotations.label: Translates map Borel sets to Borel sets
annotations.note: In a topological group with `BorelSpace` and `MeasurableMul`, multiplication by a constant is measurable, hence sends Borel sets to Borel sets.

annotations.label: Singular and simplicial chains
annotations.note: `singularChainComplexFunctor` builds the singular chain complex of a topological space, with simplicial chains underneath via `SSet.chainComplexFunctor`.

annotations.label: Sum of five non-zero squares
annotations.note: Lagrange's four-square theorem (sum_four_squares) is formalized, but the distinct result on sums of five non-zero squares is not.
